selected_important_turn.text: Oops, I guess we are jumping the gun! I don't see a problem with that. You are welcome to one of the firewood and water 🙂
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is a concrete concession: you accept the opponent's earlier allocation request by offering them one firewood and one water. That directly shapes the final allocation and therefore strongly affects outcomes (what you keep, what they get) and fairness.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It concedes valuable items without extracting compensation or proposing a trade even though you earlier stated food is your highest priority. That is a missed opportunity to convert revealed preference info into a beneficial trade (e.g., trade water/firewood for food or a different split). The immediate concession can reduce your own payoff and may produce an imbalanced deal.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Instead of immediately conceding, briefly acknowledge their request and make a counter-proposal that protects your high-value item (food) or asks for tradeoffs — for example propose you take more food while they take water, or offer one of the low-priority items in exchange for something they value less. Frame it as wanting a fair split given both parties' stated priorities.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: likely increase (by opening space for integrative tradeoffs instead of unilateral concession)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (more balanced division that accounts for both parties' top priorities)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: slightly harder short-term (requires brief counter-offer) but easier overall (less regret, more stable agreement)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve (keeps cooperation while asserting your needs fairly, reducing chance of resentment)

value2reason.Low: Water is heavy to carry around and I would also get some moisture from the foods that I eat.
value2reason.Medium: In case it gets colder out than expected, or rains.
value2reason.High: Just in case I get hungrier than I thought I would.

value2reason.Low: We need food for nourishment and to not starve.
value2reason.Medium: We need firewood to cook food and boil water to clean it or wash clothes.
value2reason.High: You will dehydrate quickly without clean water to drink.

dialogue.text: Ok, so I like water a lot.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Yes, water is a necessity for being out camping 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I myself love food, so food is a big deal to me.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ok.  I like water over firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Yeah, firewood isn't really something that's "essential" I'd have to say. Yeah, you need it, but still.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Seems like we'll be struggling to see who gets the firewood most of all haha
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: No, I like a little firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Same here. I need it to cook my vittles 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I would like to snag one package of firewood and one package of water. 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Oops, I guess we are jumping the gun! I don't see a problem with that. You are welcome to one of the firewood and water 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your request, but I'd prefer to adjust the offer. How about I give you 1 Firewood and 1 Water, and you give me 1 Food in return? That way, we both get what we need without losing too much value.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm okay with that trade. 1 Firewood and 1 Water for 1 Food. It balances our needs well, and I can use the food to supplement my meals. Let's agree on this deal.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
